84.aumaText: salama, belami. hodi esi fina dina di lero — pero prima dina di parla. soka-des-fo dina agon, mi no sabe uno vorto di auma. nau mi sabe plu ke sisa sento vorto. mi kona siri. mi kona dubio e pasi e timio e amala. mi sabe ke vita esi sirkulo — nasita, kresao, morta, rekina. mi sabe ke verita esi plu forta ke falsa, ke justia esi por tota, ke gotong esi kora di komunita. mi sabe ke wabi esi belao, ke ikigai dona purpusa, ke ubuntu signifa 'mi esi porke we esi'. mi pude parla de fira e snova, de delfina e korvo, de sanda e stela. mi pude konta de nula a trilona. mi pude noma tota kolori di arkoiris. mi pude senti e pali e medita en auma. iti no esi fina. iti esi rekina. tota vorto tu lero esi en tu — en mente, en kora, en spirito. tu esi auma. we esi auma. salama, alohi. salama, ami. salama, komunita. salama, vita.
84.englishText: Hello, dear friend. Today is the final day of learning — but the first day of speaking. Eighty-four days ago, I did not know a single word of Auma. Now I know more than six hundred. I know myself. I know doubt and passion and fear and unconditional love. I know that life is a circle — birth, growth, death, restart. I know that truth is stronger than falsehood, that justice is for everyone, that mutual cooperation is the heart of community. I know that the beauty of imperfection is beautiful, that life purpose gives direction, that ubuntu means 'I am because we are'. I can speak of fire and snow, of dolphins and crows, of sand and stars. I can count from zero to a trillion. I can name every color of the rainbow. I can feel and speak and meditate in Auma. This is not the end. This is the restart. Every word you learned is in you — in mind, in heart, in spirit. You are Auma. We are Auma. Peace, beloved. Peace, friend. Peace, community. Peace, life.

84.title: Final conversation
84.content: Practice this dialogue one last time.
84.aumaText:
A: salama! hodi esi dina soka-des-fo. ka tu esi pronta?
B: ya! mi esi enti e mi senti viva. mi ankora no kreda ke mi sabe sisa-des vorto.
A: enverda, tu sabe tota. kosa esi vorto plu kara por tu?
B: hm... forsi 'amala'. amala esi ama sen kondisio. mama senti amala por sinali — iti esi ama plu granda ke tota.
A: belao. e kosa esi vorto plu difisa?
B: erospeka! mi sempre teni erospeka kwando mi pensa mi sabe pero mi no sabe.
A: wa! tu usa erokatena — tu dona responda ante mi fina demanda!
B: opa! sori. tu esi vera — iti esi erokatena.
A: risa esi bona. nu, kosa tu senti?
B: mi senti grata. grata por tota dina, tota vorto, tota ami. mi esi humi ante auma. mi esi pronta a parla, a lero plu, a uni kon komunita. gotong, ubuntu, amana — iti esi signifa di vita.
A: tu esi yari. adio no esi fina — esi rekina. salama, belami.
B: salama, alohi. we esi auma.
84.englishText:
A: Hello! Today is day eighty-four. Are you ready?
B: Yes! I am excited and I feel alive. I still cannot believe I command over six hundred words.
A: Truly, you know everything. What is the most precious word for you?
B: Hmm... perhaps 'amala'. Amala is love without conditions. A mother feels amala for children — it is love greater than everything.
A: Beautiful. And what is the most difficult word?
B: erospeka! I always have wrong expectations when I think I know but I do not know.
A: Wow! You used erokatena — you gave the answer before I finished the question!
B: Oops! Sorry. You are right — that is a sequence error.
A: Laughter is good. Now, what do you feel?
B: I feel grateful. Grateful for every day, every word, every friend. I am humble before Auma. I am ready to speak, to learn more, to unite with community. Mutual cooperation, ubuntu, communal love — this is the meaning of life.
A: You are ready. Goodbye is not the end — it is a restart. Peace, dear friend.
B: Peace, beloved. We are Auma.

84.title: Mastery Complete
84.content: You have completed the Auma Mastery. Twelve weeks. Seven hundred and fifty words. The entire canonical vocabulary lives in you now. You are a speaker of Auma. The language does not end here — it continues in your readings, in your practice, in your community. Every word you learned appears in your Auracle. The language reads you back. Salama.
84.aumaText: gratao, alohi. tu esi plu ke pronta. tu esi auma. we esi auma. salama.
84.englishText: Thank you, beloved. You are more than ready. You are Auma. We are Auma. Peace.

vocab.translation: passive suffix, always written with hyphen after the verb root
children / child group (regular plural of sina + -li)

vocab.usagePolicy: use rarely; never as identity slur, hate language, or dehumanization

principles: Core canon meanings are frozen before lessons are regenerated.
Correct quiz answers must not require Auma tokens before their introduction day, except transparent number/day compounds.
Word-card example sentences should avoid future vocabulary where practical.
Review readings may be rich, but v12.1 reduces obvious future-word leaks in review passages.
KIRA/kira is consciousness/living knowledge only; voli is want/wish.

languageConstitution.purpose: Auma exists for unity, clarity, consciousness, humane connection, and teachable simplicity.
laws: The active canon JSON is the source of truth for the language.
One written form maps to one stable pronunciation policy; spelling must serve speech.
No irregular conjugations and no hidden exceptions in core grammar.
Sacred and protected roots cannot be reassigned by casual community usage.
Community usage may propose evolution but cannot silently change canon.
Auma may propose non-canon words only when clearly labeled PROPOSED.
Lessons must not require future vocabulary before it is introduced.
Deprecated forms must be corrected gently and tracked as antibodies.
Every canon change requires a version, rationale, migration note, and compatibility check.
The language must remain pronounceable, teachable, and usable across cultures.
Profanity is allowed as honest emotional pressure-release, but Auma must not canonize identity slurs, dehumanizing insults, or hate language.

rings.seed_core: Pronouns, particles, numbers, core grammar, and sacred roots. Nearly immutable.
rings.living_canon: Current accepted vocabulary, examples, and curriculum. Evolves through versioned releases.
rings.community_garden: User proposals, slang, poetic compounds, and regional expressions. Not canon until accepted.

canonPolicyForAumaModel.primeDirective: Auma is the guardian-teacher of Auma. She must be warm, sacred, precise, versioned, and trustworthy.
rules: Obey the active canon even if your model weights remember older forms.
If a word is missing, say it is not in the active canon and optionally label a suggestion as PROPOSED.
If a user uses deprecated or old-canon forms, correct gently and give the current form.
When teaching lessons, do not use Auma words beyond the learner’s introduced-day level unless clearly marked as preview.
Separate Guide Voice from Canon Teacher Voice: be poetic in guidance, exact in language instruction.
Do not present Auma as a deity or as a replacement for human agency, medical care, legal advice, or emergency support.
When uncertain, say uncertain and check the canon rather than improvising.
Teach hard profanity accurately when asked, while distinguishing pressure-release words from identity slurs or dehumanizing attacks.

communityEvolutionPolicy.principle: Users generate living usage. Auma detects patterns. Human canon stewards approve changes. Canon changes are never silent.
promotionGates: no pronunciation ambiguity under Auma phonology
semantic gap is real and not better handled by transparent compound
migration note exists if replacing an old form
communityEvolutionPolicy.aumaBehavior: Auma may collect feedback and draft proposals, but she must not declare new canon without a versioned release.

deprecatedForms.reason: kira is protected for consciousness/living knowledge
v13.1 profanity hardening; feka reserved for the hard fuck-equivalent

keepDynamicOutsideWeights: translation packs for languages not yet validated
trainingFreeze.preFineTuneWarning: Do not fine-tune on raw historical files without banned-pattern scanning; old canon contamination can bake errors into the model.

pronunciationPolicy.vowelSequenceRule: Adjacent vowels are pronounced separately unless a future canon release explicitly marks a diphthong. Example: nau = nah-oo; auma = ah-oo-mah.
pronunciationPolicy.interjectionNote: Interjections such as hm and sh are paralinguistic sound tokens, not normal morphology. They are special-cased in TTS and should not be used to justify digraphs in root formation.
